name: Eggnog Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease an 8-inch square pan and lightly grease the sides of a heavy, medium saucepan.
2. In the prepared saucepan, combine eggnog, corn syrup, butter (or margarine), and sugar. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar is dissolved and the mixture comes to a boil. Brush the sides of the pan frequently with a pastry brush dipped in hot water to remove sugar crystals.
3. Add a candy thermometer. Continue cooking until the mixture reaches the soft-ball stage (238°F).
4. Remove from heat and pour into a large, heat-proof mixing bowl. Cool to lukewarm (about 110°F), then stir in vanilla. Return to the heavy-duty mixer and beat until thick. Spread into the prepared pan.
5. Score the fudge into squares with a knife. Refrigerate until firm. Cut into squares. Makes about 1 pound.
